parameters-en.Gain 1: Controls the amount of Gain 1 drive applied to the signal.
parameters-en.Tone 1: Controls the overall tonal balance of Gain 1. Lower values are darker and higher values are brighter.
parameters-en.Presence 1: Adds a bit more high end sparkle to Gain 1.
parameters-en.Clipping 1: Selects the clipping mode for Gain 1. "Overdrive" has a touch of drive and compression. "Clean" is less compressed than "Overdrive" and is ideal for boosting your signal into an amp; it's the default setting for Gain 1. "Distortion" provides even more drive and compression.
parameters-en.Gain Mod 1: The original pedal has different variations available, one with more gain ("Higher").
parameters-en.Level 1: Sets the overall output level of Gain 1.
parameters-en.Gain 2: Controls the amount of Gain 2 drive applied to the signal.
parameters-en.Tone 2: Controls the overall tonal balance of Gain 2. Lower values are darker and higher values are brighter.
parameters-en.Presence 2: Adds a bit more high end sparkle to Gain 2.
parameters-en.Clipping 2: Selects the clipping mode for Gain 2. "Overdrive" has a touch of drive and compression; it's the default setting for Gain 2. "Clean" is less compressed than "Overdrive" and is ideal for boosting your signal into an amp. "Distortion" provides even more drive and compression.
parameters-en.Gain Mod 2: The original pedal has different variations available, one with more gain ("Higher").
parameters-en.Level 2: Sets the overall output level of Gain 2.
parameters-en.Voltage: The original pedal was designed to be powered by a 9V power supply but could alternatively accommodate 18V, which would provide a bit more headroom. This is especially apparent when Clipping is set to "Boost" and Gain is turned up.
